full_content:
Batch formalization: GT-KB core application specification intake Phase 0.

Owner approval:
- Mike approved continuing the GTKB-CORE-001 workstream after reviewing the standing backlog continuation.
- Mike approved proceeding from the Phase 0 governance and compatibility proposal in bridge/gtkb-core-spec-intake-001.md.

Artifacts to create:
- DELIB-0875: owner decision approving the core specification intake workstream and default-on/opt-out direction.
- SPEC-CORE-INTAKE-001: GT-KB prompts for missing core application specifications after project initialization.
- SPEC-CORE-INTAKE-002: Core specification prompting ceases once all required core slots are owner-stated or explicitly not applicable.
- ADR-CORE-INTAKE-001: Core spec completion is derived from persisted MemBase evidence, not session memory.
- DCL-CORE-INTAKE-001: Existing scaffold profile behavior and non-interactive workflows remain backward-compatible while core intake becomes default-on with an explicit opt-out.

Approved compatibility policy:
- New projects should be enrolled in core-spec prompting by default.
- Automation and unusual projects require an explicit opt-out path.
- Non-interactive and JSON-safe command paths must not emit interactive prompts.
- Existing minimal/full scaffold semantics must remain backward-compatible unless separately approved.
- Inferred candidates do not count as complete until owner-confirmed or marked not applicable.

Implementation boundary:
- This approval authorizes Phase 0 formalization and the next implementation proposal.
- It does not itself implement GT-KB package code.
- Later package changes still require review, tests, and post-implementation verification.
change_reason: Formalize GT-KB core application specification intake Phase 0 governance and compatibility policy.
